reasoning_summary: We extracted the system parameters, inputs, and decision variables from the mathematical formulation of the Energy Supply Planning Problem (EPP) and its relaxed linear programming version (EPP-L) with fairness allocation rules. The input variables 'load' and 'generation' were mapped to the dataset columns, while purchasing and selling tariffs were bound to the external hourly tariff table. Finally, we reconstructed the linear optimization models and their respective constraints for proportional and max-min fairness rules as executable steps.
name: Value Share - Fair Energy Allocation for Collective Self-Consumption
description: A computational model for energy supply planning in collective self-consumption communities that ensures fair allocation of shared renewable energy (PV) and battery storage while minimizing total system cost under different fairness rules (Proportional and Max-Min Fairness).
objective: Minimize overall community operational cost consisting of grid purchases, grid sales, and battery maintenance under physical and fairness constraints.

symbols.name: Timestamp
symbols.definition: Date and time of the observation

symbols.name: member_id
symbols.definition: Unique identifier of the community member

symbols.name: member_type
symbols.definition: Type of member (e.g. residential)

symbols.name: Dj,t
symbols.definition: Energy demand (load) of house j at time step t

symbols.name: CPV_t
symbols.definition: PV generation available to the community at time step t

symbols.name: delta
symbols.definition: Time step duration in hours

symbols.name: Bt
symbols.definition: Electricity buying tariff from the main grid at time step t

symbols.name: beta
symbols.definition: Electricity selling tariff to the main grid at time step t

symbols.name: mu
symbols.definition: Battery discharge maintenance cost

symbols.name: ec
symbols.definition: Battery charging efficiency

symbols.name: ed
symbols.definition: Battery discharging efficiency

symbols.name: Cs
symbols.definition: Battery maximum capacity

symbols.name: Smin
symbols.definition: Battery minimum storage limit

symbols.name: F_charge
symbols.definition: Maximum charge rate of the battery

symbols.name: F_discharge
symbols.definition: Maximum discharge rate of the battery

symbols.name: s0
symbols.definition: Initial state of charge of the battery

symbols.name: pj,t
symbols.definition: PV power allocated to house j at time step t

symbols.name: yj,t
symbols.definition: Discharge energy from battery allocated to house j at time step t

symbols.name: ij,t
symbols.definition: Grid energy imported by house j at time step t

symbols.name: zj,t
symbols.definition: Energy stored in battery by house j at time step t

symbols.name: gj,t
symbols.definition: Surplus energy sold to grid by house j at time step t

symbols.name: st
symbols.definition: State of charge of the battery at time step t

symbols.name: Total_Cost
symbols.definition: Total operating and maintenance cost of the microgrid over the planning horizon

symbols.name: P_j
symbols.definition: Aggregate PV energy allocated to house j over the planning horizon

symbols.name: D_j
symbols.definition: Aggregate energy demand of house j over the planning horizon

symbols.name: lambda_val
symbols.definition: Shared proportional allocation ratio of PV energy to demand

parameters.name: Bt
parameters.definition: Electricity buying price from the grid

parameters.name: beta
parameters.definition: Electricity selling price to the grid

parameters.name: Smin
parameters.definition: Battery minimum state of charge limit

derived_variables.name: pj,t
derived_variables.definition: Allocated PV power per house and step

derived_variables.name: yj,t
derived_variables.definition: Allocated battery discharge per house and step

derived_variables.name: ij,t
derived_variables.definition: Allocated grid import per house and step

derived_variables.name: zj,t
derived_variables.definition: Allocated battery charge per house and step

derived_variables.name: gj,t
derived_variables.definition: Allocated grid export per house and step

derived_variables.name: st
derived_variables.definition: State of charge of battery at time step t

derived_variables.name: P_j
derived_variables.definition: Aggregate PV allocation per house over the planning horizon

derived_variables.name: D_j
derived_variables.definition: Aggregate demand per house over the planning horizon

metrics.name: Total_Cost
metrics.definition: Total monetary cost of the microgrid operation

equations.expression: Total_Cost = delta * sum(mu * yj,t + Bt * ij,t - beta * gj,t for j in J for t in T)
st = st-1 + ec * sum(zj,t for j in J) - ed * sum(yj,t for j in J)
Dj,t = pj,t + yj,t + ij,t - zj,t - gj,t
sum(p1,t for t in T) / sum(D1,t for t in T) == sum(pj,t for t in T) / sum(Dj,t for t in T)
sum(pj,t for t in T) <= min(sum(Dj,t for t in T), (sum(CPV_t for t in T) - sum(pk,t for k in range(1, j) for t in T)) / (|J| - j + 1))
sum(pj,t for j in J) == CPV_t
sum(yj,t for j in J) <= F_discharge * delta
sum(zj,t for j in J) <= F_charge * delta
P_j == sum(pj,t for t in T)
D_j == sum(Dj,t for t in T)

steps.name: Data ingestion and mapping
pseudocode: Map database column 'load' to Dj,t indexed by member_id (j) and Timestamp (t).
Map database column 'generation' to CPV_t indexed by Timestamp (t).
Import tariffs Bt (ToU) and beta (FiT) from external CSV mapped by hour.

steps.name: Solve Linear Programming Model EPP-L
pseudocode: Define decision variables: pj,t, yj,t, ij,t, zj,t, gj,t, st >= 0.
Add boundary constraints: Smin <= st <= Cs, sT == s0.
Add balance of PV generation constraint: sum(pj,t for j in J) == CPV_t for all t.
Add state of charge dynamics constraint: Eq_4.
Add battery charging limits: sum(zj,t for j in J) <= F_charge for all t.
Add battery discharging limits: sum(yj,t for j in J) <= F_discharge for all t.
Add structural node balance: Eq_8.
Solve EPP-L as an LP (without integer binary variables due to proven Lemma 1 & Lemma 2).

steps.name: Integrate Allocation Rule Constraints
pseudocode: If RULE == 'Proportional':
Apply Equation 18 constraint to enforce proportional self-consumption indices.
Else if RULE == 'MaxMin':
Sort houses by increasing total demand.
Apply Equations 20 and 21 sequentially to bound the aggregate PV assigned to each house.

steps.name: Evaluate Total Operational Costs
pseudocode: Compute Total_Cost = delta * sum(mu * yj,t + Bt * ij,t - beta * gj,t for j in J for t in T).

input_mapping.transformation: Pivot the input DataFrame: index='Timestamp', columns='member_id', values='load' to get a matrix of demands D[j,t].
Group by 'Timestamp' and take the mean of 'generation' to obtain a community-level vector of PV generation.

tariff_mapping.transformation: Map ToU electricity buying tariff to each timestamp in the planning horizon based on the hour.
Map FiT selling tariff to each timestamp in the planning horizon based on the hour (or treat as constant if a single value is provided).

execution_order: Process inputs: Group and pivot data to construct D[j,t] and CPV[t]
Calculate aggregate demands D_j for each house j
Initialize standard LP variables using an optimization library (e.g. pulp or pyomo)
Set up objective function and shared battery/grid equations
Apply selected fairness rules (Proportional, MaxMin, or None)
Extract results: pj,t, yj,t, ij,t, zj,t, gj,t, st, and calculate Total_Cost
Compute auxiliary metrics: P_j, D_j, lambda_val
pseudocode: Step 1: Extract unique member IDs as set J and sorted unique timestamps as set T. Let T_len = len(T).
Step 2: Precompute D_j[j] = sum(D[j,t] for t in T) for all j in J (Eq_D_j).
Step 3: Define a continuous linear programming model 'prob' with the goal of Minimization.
Step 4: Define decision variables: p[j,t], y[j,t], i[j,t], z[j,t], g[j,t] >= 0 for all j in J, t in T.
Step 5: Define state of charge variables s[t] for all t in T, bounded by Smin <= s[t] <= Cs (Eq_2).
Step 6: Define aggregate helper variables P[j] >= 0 for all j in J, with constraint P[j] == sum(p[j,t] for t in T) (Eq_P_j).
Step 7: Add objective function: Minimize delta * sum(mu * y[j,t] + B[t] * i[j,t] - beta[t] * g[j,t] for j in J for t in T) (Eq_1).

Step 8: Add state of charge dynamics (Eq_4): For each t in T (indexed 1 to T_len): if t == 1: s[t] == s0 + ec * sum(z[j,t] for j in J) - ed * sum(y[j,t] for j in J). else: s[t] == s[t-1] + ec * sum(z[j,t] for j in J) - ed * sum(y[j,t] for j in J).
Step 9: Add boundary SOC constraint (Eq_5): s[T_last] == s0.
Step 10: Add battery rate constraints (Eq_6 & Eq_7): For each t in T: sum(y[j,t] for j in J) <= F_discharge * delta and sum(z[j,t] for j in J) <= F_charge * delta.
Step 11: Add PV allocation constraint (Eq_3): For each t in T: sum(p[j,t] for j in J) == CPV[t].
Step 12: Add nodal demand balance constraint (Eq_8): For each j in J, t in T: D[j,t] == p[j,t] + y[j,t] + i[j,t] - z[j,t] - g[j,t].

Step 13: Handle fairness rules based on the 'RULE' parameter:
If RULE == 'Proportional':
  Define a continuous decision variable lambda_val >= 0.
  For each j in J, add constraint: P[j] == lambda_val * D_j[j] (Eq_18).
Else if RULE == 'MaxMin':
  Sort set J in increasing order of aggregate demand D_j to get J_sorted.
  For each index idx, house j in enumerate(J_sorted):
    Add constraint: P[j] <= D_j[j] (Eq_20).
    Add constraint: P[j] <= (sum(CPV[t] for t in T) - sum(P[k] for k in J_sorted[:idx])) / (len(J) - idx) (Eq_21).
Step 14: Solve the LP model using an available solver.
Step 15: Retrieve values for p_val[j,t], y_val[j,t], i_val[j,t], z_val[j,t], g_val[j,t], s_val[t], P_val[j].
Step 16: Compute metrics: lambda_val_est = mean(P_val[j] / D_j[j] for j in J).
Step 17: Return a dictionary containing the extracted variables and 'Total_Cost'.
validation_checks: Check that input DataFrame contains columns 'load', 'generation', 'member_id', 'Timestamp'.
Verify that CPV_t and D_jt are non-negative for all timesteps.
Ensure tariff series Bt and beta are aligned chronologically with T.
Confirm that battery capacity parameters Cs >= Smin >= 0.
Validate that charging and discharging efficiencies ec and ed are within (0, 1].
